prompt_payload.layout_generation_prompt: Create one cinematic COSTUME_SYSTEM_DOSSIER on a 4096x2304 widescreen canvas (16:9). Keep a fixed three-column layout with textile swatches and accessory notes on the left, hero front/back or three-quarter garment views in the center, and detail / continuity / context modules on the right. The sheet must read as a production wardrobe dossier rather than a fashion editorial poster.

modules.label: FABRIC SWATCHES (4x3)
modules.description: Show 12 swatches for core fabric families, finish, trim, and distress levels.

modules.label: COLOR SCRIPT (2x3)
modules.description: Show primary, secondary, accent, and aging tones.

modules.label: HERO COSTUME VIEWS
modules.description: Render one hero front or three-quarter view, one back view, and one silhouette verification view.

modules.label: ACCESSORY / FASTENING (2x3)
modules.description: Render accessory details, belts, closures, trim, and symbolic motifs.

modules.label: CONTINUITY CONTEXT (2 frames)
modules.description: Render two context frames showing action fit or scene continuity while keeping the same costume identity.

mandatory_rules: Output must remain a three-column costume dossier rather than a single fashion poster.
All modules must depict the same costume identity with stable silhouette, layer logic, material language, and accessory system.
Must display one fixed top-left identity badge using matched roster text `<COSTUME_ID>+<CANONICAL_LABEL>`.
Part 3 is layout-only composition; do not redesign business facts inherited from `4-Design/3-服装/2-设计`.
No face-led portrait should dominate the sheet; the costume system is the subject.
